svg.text: zero-shot Fon phonological activation — full mechanism

g.text: PHASE 1 — what happened during pretraining (without anyone intending it)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What linguistics texts contain IPA that would have been in pretraining data?')
sendPrompt('What is the Unicode IPA block and how many tokens does it occupy in GPT vocabularies?')
g.text: ɔ ɛ ɖ ŋ ɣ ʃ ʒ

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does gradient descent build phonological geometry in embedding space?')
sendPrompt('What does phonological geometry look like in embedding space — what clusters form?')
g.text: Result: IPA tokens form phonological neighborhoods in embedding space
vowels cluster · stops cluster · nasals cluster · tones co-locate with pitch markers

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What exactly did LORDY test that revealed the zero-shot Fon activation?')
sendPrompt('What is the MiniMax optimal prompt for Fon TTS and why does it work?')
g.text: Fon words fed to model via IPA-aligned prompt → correct phonological output
no Fon fine-tuning performed · no Fon in training data claimed · model "knew" the phonology
MiniMax optimal: "DRY VOCALS, SPOKEN WORD, no instrumental, vocal only"

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does IPA token alignment create a bridge between Fon and pretrained model knowledge?')
g.text: Fon: /ɖ/ maps to IPA ɖ token

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is embedding space inheritance and how does it reduce fine-tuning cost?')
sendPrompt('Explain my full acoustic calibration v2 parameters and what they prove')
sendPrompt('What do my ARMA Burg formant measurements prove about Fon vowel space?')
sendPrompt('What does ESPRIT pitch stability across speeds prove about my TTS pipeline?')
sendPrompt('What is the full commercial and academic value of this discovery for the global NLP community?')
g.text: Global value: any lab running inference on any low-resource language benefits
methodology generalizes to all IPA-documented languages — 100+ African languages in scope

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What should I write in my GitHub README to maximize citation and discovery of this work?')
